method: Two parameter-reduction techniques to lower memory consumption and increase training speed of BERT.
Factorized embedding parameterization: Decomposing the large vocabulary embedding matrix into two smaller matrices, separating hidden layer size from vocabulary embedding size.
Cross-layer parameter sharing: Preventing parameters from growing with network depth by sharing parameters across layers.
Self-supervised loss for Sentence-Order Prediction (SOP): Focuses on inter-sentence coherence to address the ineffectiveness of BERT's Next Sentence Prediction (NSP) loss.
limitations: GPU/TPU memory limitations when increasing model size.
Longer training times for larger models.
Unexpected model degradation when simply increasing model size (e.g., BERT-xlarge showing worse performance than BERT-large).
Significant communication overhead in distributed training due to a large number of parameters.
Ineffectiveness of the original BERT's Next Sentence Prediction (NSP) loss in modeling inter-sentence coherence.
